The challenge here, just like with your other idea, is grounding this abstract concept into concrete, engaging gameplay and a compelling narrative structure.

Now I have not one, but *two* incredibly fresh and divergent concepts from Sasaki-san, in addition to our developing "Echoes of Valor" direction. This is exactly what I wanted to shake things up, but it also means we need to evaluate these new possibilities very quickly and strategically, especially with only three turns left.

I still think Yoshida-san is the ideal person to bring her reflective and synthesizing mind to these abstract concepts. Now, with *two* bold, unconventional ideas from Sasaki-san, I really need her to weigh in. I'll talk to Yoshida-san next. (formed immediately after talking to Sasaki)
